data.title: Shanghai Pioneer Holding Declares Final Dividend for 2025
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shanghai Pioneer Holding Ltd ( (HK:1345) ) just unveiled an update.

Shanghai Pioneer Holding Ltd has proposed a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.054 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 22 May 2026. The dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 18 June 2026, with the ex-dividend date set for 3 June and a book close period from 5 to 9 June to determine shareholder entitlement.

The announcement underscores the company’s intention to return cash to shareholders, signaling confidence in its 2025 financial performance and ongoing cash-generating ability. Key dates around the ex-dividend period and record date will be closely watched by investors seeking to capture the payout, while the absence of withholding tax on the declared dividend simplifies the return for eligible shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1345) stock is a Buy with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shanghai Pioneer Holding Ltd stock, see the HK:1345 Stock Forecast page.

Shanghai Pioneer Holding Ltd is a Hong Kong-listed equity issuer, trading under stock code 01345. The company is monitored by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard corporate governance practices for listed firms in the region.

data.title: NSING Technologies Sets Up Board-Level Strategic and ESG Committee
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NSING Technologies Inc. Class H ( (HK:2701) ) has issued an announcement.

NSING Technologies Inc. has created a Strategic and ESG Committee under its board of directors to oversee long-term development strategy, major investment decisions and environmental, social and corporate governance matters. The committee is designed to enhance strategic planning, improve the quality and rationality of investment decisions, deepen sustainability-focused reforms and refine the company’s corporate governance structure.

The new body will operate independently of other internal departments, report directly to the board and be composed of three directors led by the company’s chairman. Its establishment aligns the company with Chinese and Hong Kong listing and governance requirements, clarifying director responsibilities and ensuring continuity and proper replacement procedures so the committee can function effectively in guiding strategy and ESG management.

More about NSING Technologies Inc. Class H

NSING Technologies Inc. operates as a publicly listed company subject to corporate governance rules in both mainland China and Hong Kong. The company is focused on strengthening its long-term strategic development, core competitiveness and investment decision-making processes, while integrating environmental, social and corporate governance practices into its management and oversight framework.

Current Market Cap: HK$9.09B

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Greentown Management Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:9979) ) has provided an announcement.

Greentown Management Holdings Company Limited has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.0916 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with the Hong Kong dollar equivalent and applicable exchange rate to be announced later. The dividend is subject to shareholder approval on 26 June 2026, with shares trading ex-dividend on 6 July 2026, a record date of 13 July 2026, and payment scheduled for 17 July 2026, signaling the company’s intention to return cash to shareholders and maintain a regular distribution policy.

The book closure period for determining dividend entitlement will run from 8 July to 13 July 2026, during which share transfers must be lodged with Computershare Hong Kong Investor Services Limited by the specified deadline. The announcement, which involves no withholding tax or related warrant and convertible security adjustments, provides clarity on the company’s capital distribution timetable and offers income visibility to investors amid ongoing scrutiny of returns in the property management sector.

data.title: Anjoy Foods Grows Revenue, Expands Assets and Overseas Ambitions Despite Profit Dip
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Anjoy Foods Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2648) ).

Anjoy Foods reported 2025 operating income of RMB16.19 billion, up 7.05% year on year, while net profit attributable to shareholders fell 8.46% to RMB1.36 billion, amid a 19.5% expansion in total assets and a similar rise in net assets. The board proposed a final dividend of RMB1.44 per share, signalling continued shareholder returns despite margin pressure.

Management highlighted strong market response to new product lines such as hot-pot assortments, shrimp paste and premium sausage ranges, supported by a strategy of continuous product iteration and channel optimization. The group is deepening cooperation with new retail platforms, pushing omnichannel distribution, and leveraging social media to shape consumption scenarios and drive demand.

Anjoy is also pursuing aggressive growth initiatives, including the acquisition of Jiangsu Ding Wei Tai to build a frozen bakery division and the launch of the “Anzhai” project targeting domestic and overseas Halal food markets. Following its July 2025 Hong Kong Main Board listing, which made it China’s first A+H listed quick-frozen food company, Anjoy is using the H-share platform to advance its internationalization, including export growth and potential overseas M&A, reinforcing its leadership in the domestic quick-frozen food industry.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2648) stock is a Buy with a HK$91.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anjoy Foods Group Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2648 Stock Forecast page.

More about Anjoy Foods Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Anjoy Foods Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based quick-frozen food producer, focusing on customized, high-quality products for mass consumers in the domestic market. The company has built standardized, scalable operations and an omnichannel sales network spanning traditional distributors, supermarkets, new retail platforms, and e-commerce, while also accelerating international expansion through its A+H listing structure.

Average Trading Volume: 580,266

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$28.95B

An announcement from China Huarong Asset Management Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2799) ) is now available.

China CITIC Financial Asset Management has released its audited annual results for the year ended December 31, 2025, fulfilling Hong Kong listing requirements for preliminary annual disclosures. The company will distribute printed versions of its 2025 annual report in April 2026 to H-share holders who opted in, and will also publish the full report on both the Hong Kong stock exchange website and its own corporate site.

The forthcoming annual report will provide detailed financial statements, management discussion and analysis, risk and capital management disclosures and corporate governance reports, offering investors a comprehensive view of the firm’s 2025 performance and oversight framework. This level of disclosure is expected to support transparency for stakeholders and help the market assess the company’s operational resilience, risk profile and strategic outlook within China’s evolving financial asset management sector.

data.title: Shanghai Biren Tech Reshapes Board With Independent Director Change
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Shanghai Biren Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6082) ).

Shanghai Biren Technology announced the resignation of independent non-executive director Dr. Yuan Wang, who will step down from the board and its key committees following the election of a successor at the upcoming annual general meeting. The company emphasized that Dr. Wang’s departure is due to work arrangements, with no disagreements with the board or issues requiring shareholder attention.

The board has proposed appointing microelectronics expert Dr. Zhiyi Yu as an independent non-executive director and member of the audit, nomination and remuneration committees, subject to approval at the AGM. His appointment, backed by extensive IC and AI chip R&D credentials and an annual fee of RMB700,000, is intended to strengthen Biren’s governance and technical oversight as it advances its semiconductor strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6082) stock is a Buy with a HK$54.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shanghai Biren Technology Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:6082 Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Changmao Biochemical Engineering Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:0954) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Changmao Biochemical Engineering Company Limited reported 2025 revenue of RMB556.9 million, down slightly from a year earlier, and a loss attributable to shareholders of RMB66.8 million, broadly in line with 2024. The board has decided not to recommend a final dividend for the year, underscoring continued earnings pressure.

Despite modestly improving gross profit and lower administrative expenses that narrowed the operating loss, higher finance costs weighed on the bottom line and kept the group in the red. The results highlight ongoing profitability challenges and balance-sheet pressure for the biochemical producer, with shareholders facing another year without a cash payout as management prioritises financial stability.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0954) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Changmao Biochemical Engineering Co. Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:0954 Stock Forecast page.

Changmao Biochemical Engineering Company Limited is a PRC-incorporated biochemical producer listed in Hong Kong, operating in the specialty chemicals sector. The group manufactures and sells biochemical products, with revenue largely derived from industrial customers, and is exposed to raw material costs, financing expenses and demand trends in its niche markets.

data.title: NSING Technologies Narrows 2025 Loss as Revenue and Margins Improve
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NSING Technologies Inc. Class H ( (HK:2701) ) has issued an announcement.

NSING Technologies Inc. reported a 16.5% year-on-year increase in revenue to RMB1.36 billion for 2025, driven mainly by sales of goods and services, while gross profit rose 36.1% to RMB248.3 million, lifting gross margin to 18.3%. The company significantly narrowed its annual loss to RMB117.6 million from RMB255.7 million as administrative and research expenses declined and other losses were reduced, signaling improving operating efficiency despite continued finance costs.

Earnings per share improved, with basic loss per share shrinking to RMB0.2 from RMB0.4, reflecting the reduced loss attributable to shareholders. The results indicate that, although the group remains loss-making, its financial performance is on a clear recovery path, which may strengthen stakeholder confidence in its efforts to enhance profitability and better manage costs.

More about NSING Technologies Inc. Class H

NSING Technologies Inc. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. The group is engaged in the sale of goods and services, with a revenue model that also includes lease income, positioning it within the broader technology and industrial solutions market in China.

data.title: Hepalink 2025 Profit Falls on API Weakness Despite Modest Revenue Growth
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Hepalink Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9989) ) has shared an update.

Shenzhen Hepalink Pharmaceutical Group reported 2025 revenue of RMB5.44 billion, up slightly from a year earlier, with growth driven mainly by its finished dose pharmaceutical products business, whose sales rose to RMB3.50 billion and achieved a higher gross margin. However, group gross profit slipped to RMB1.68 billion and the overall gross margin narrowed as the active pharmaceutical ingredients segment suffered a sharp revenue decline and margin compression, while CDMO sales were flat but somewhat more profitable.

Net profit attributable to shareholders dropped to RMB349.5 million as other income fell sharply and impairment losses and operating expenses weighed on earnings, though finance costs decreased and associates swung to a small gain. The board proposed a lower final cash dividend of RMB1.45 per ten shares, implying a higher payout ratio, signaling continued shareholder returns despite profit pressure and underscoring a strategic tilt toward finished dose products and CDMO to offset weakness in APIs.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:9989) stock is a Buy with a HK$6.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shenzhen Hepalink Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:9989 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shenzhen Hepalink Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Hepalink Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. is a China-based pharmaceutical company focused on finished dosage drugs, active pharmaceutical ingredients and contract development and manufacturing (CDMO) services. The group targets both domestic and international markets, with a portfolio spanning injectable therapies and heparin-related products across multiple therapeutic areas.
